stories.title: Caleb Wilson Scores 35 in Vegas Debut — Bulls Lose 97-96 to Boozer's Grizzlies

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Caleb Wilson dropped 35 points in his Vegas Summer League debut. Shot 7-of-11 from three. The Bulls lost 97-96 — but No. 4 just announced himself.

#Bulls #NBATwitter #NBASummerLeague

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
35 points. 7-of-11 from three. 5 rebounds. 3 blocks.

Caleb Wilson's Vegas debut: 2nd-highest scoring debut in Las Vegas Summer League history.

The Bulls lost. Wilson did not.

#Bulls #NBASummerLeague

article.headline: Caleb Wilson Scores 35 in Vegas Debut, But Cameron Boozer's Grizzlies Win 97-96

image_concepts.raw:

research.facts:
Caleb Wilson 35-Point Summer League Debut
**Sources:**
- Yahoo Sports: Wilson 35 points, Bulls lose 97-96 to Grizzlies (July 10, 2026)
- NBA.com: Caleb Wilson scores 35 in Vegas debut
- ESPN: Wilson "emotional," dominant in 35-point debut
- Chicago Sun-Times: Wilson's record debut rings in new Bulls era
- Bleacher Report: Cam Boozer/Caleb Wilson final box scores
**Key Facts:**
- Caleb Wilson (No. 4 overall pick, Chicago Bulls) scored 35 points in his Las Vegas Summer League debut on July 10
- Shot 12-of-21 from the field and 7-of-11 from three-point range; added 5 rebounds, 2 steals, 3 blocks
- The 35 points are the highest-ever for a player's first Las Vegas Summer League game since 2004, except for Marco Belinelli's 37 in 2007 (second-highest all-time)
- Despite Wilson's heroics, Bulls lost 97-96 to the Memphis Grizzlies
- Wilson's first competitive game since February 10 — his college season at North Carolina was cut short by a broken wrist
- Cameron Boozer (No. 3 pick, Memphis Grizzlies) scored 23 points, 6 rebounds, 4 assists, 1 steal, 1 block to lift the Grizzlies
- Boozer had 15 pts in Salt Lake City debut vs. OKC, 18 pts vs. Jazz, now 23 vs. Bulls
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple sources confirm game stats)
**Freshness:** Game played July 10, 2026 — one day ago. Peak relevance window.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
LeBron has been spending his summer in Akron. Shams says the Warriors are no longer "popping up" in his free agency talks. Cleveland already locked up Mitchell for $273M.

The signs are pointing home. #NBA

LeBron's 2026 free agency update:

Cavaliers — frontrunner
Warriors — fading per Shams
76ers — still possible
Heat — long shot

The Akron Kid might be going home for real.

#NBA #LeBronJames

LeBron James Free Agency — Cavaliers Frontrunner

- Yahoo Sports: Good news for Cavaliers on LeBron chances

- ESPN: What six teams can offer LeBron
- Hoops Rumors: Latest on LeBron's free agency
**Key Facts:**
- LeBron James (41) is an unrestricted free agent after informing the Lakers he will play elsewhere
- Averaged 20.9 ppg, 7.2 apg, 6.1 rpg on 51.5% shooting in 2025-26
- Rich Paul has spoken to 27 teams; race narrowed to two: Cleveland Cavaliers and Golden State Warriors
- Shams Charania reports Warriors are no longer "popping up" in LeBron's free agency talks
- LeBron has been spending time in Akron, Ohio this summer — spurring "coming home" speculation
- Cleveland has already signed Donovan Mitchell to a 4-year, $273M extension
- Rich Paul listed Philly, Miami, Minnesota, Denver as secondary options
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple credible reporters, Shams Charania quoted)
**Freshness:** Ongoing story — latest reports as of July 10-11. Still unresolved.

stories.title: Victor Wembanyama Signs 5-Year, $252M Extension With Spurs — Leaves $50M on Table

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Wembanyama agreed to a 5-year, $252M extension with the Spurs.

He could've taken the 30% supermax — roughly $303M. He took the 25% max instead. Left $50M on the table.

He believes in the rebuild.

#Spurs #NBA

article.headline: Wembanyama Signs $252M Extension With Spurs — Takes Less to Stay, Leaves $50M Behind

---

research.facts:
Victor Wembanyama $252M Extension
**Sources:**
- ESPN: Spurs, Wembanyama reach $252M max extension (July 11, 2026)
- CBS Sports: Wembanyama agrees to 5-year, $252M extension
- Bleacher Report: Wemby, Spurs agree to $252M contract
- NBC Sports: Wemby's extension leaves $50M on table
**Key Facts:**
- San Antonio Spurs and Victor Wembanyama agreed to a 5-year, $252 million max rookie-scale extension
- The extension runs through the 2031-32 season, with a player option in year five
- Wembanyama chose the 25% max rather than the 30% supermax — leaving approximately $50 million on the table
- The supermax would have been approximately $303 million
- The $252M deal is the third-largest rookie extension in NBA history, behind Cade Cunningham ($269M) and Evan Mobley ($269M)
- Wembanyama's discount signals commitment to San Antonio's long-term project
- Confidence: HIGH (ESPN, CBS Sports, Bleacher Report all confirm)
**Freshness:** Reported July 11, 2026 — breaking today.

---

research.facts:
Giannis + Jaylen Brown Trades Official
**Sources:**
- NBA.com: Heat acquire Giannis in blockbuster deal
- ESPN: Bucks trading Giannis to Heat for Herro, Ware, Jaquez, Jakucionis, picks
- NBA.com: Sixers acquire Jaylen Brown from Celtics
- ESPN: Sources — Celtics sending Brown to 76ers for George and picks
- Bleacher Report: Giannis extension options revealed
**Key Facts — Giannis Trade:**
- Milwaukee traded Giannis Antetokounmpo + Bobby Portis to Miami Heat (July 6, 2026)
- Miami sent back Tyler Herro, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kel'el Ware, Kasparas Jakučionis, pick No. 13, unprotected firsts in 2031/2033, a 2030 pick swap, and a 2033 second-rounder
- Giannis eligible for 4-year, $275 million extension starting January 6, 2027
- Miami's title odds moved from 30-1 to 18-1 at DraftKings
- Confidence: HIGH (NBA.com, ESPN confirmed)
**Key Facts — Jaylen Brown Trade:**
- Boston Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia 76ers
- Celtics received Paul George (2 years remaining on 4-yr/$212M contract), two first-round picks (2028, 2031), two second-round picks (2028, 2030)
- Brown never requested the trade
- Philadelphia now has Brown, Tyrese Maxey, Joel Embiid
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple sources confirmed)
**Freshness:** Trades became official July 6, 2026; both trade partners settled in. Follow-up angle: What does this mean for title odds?

- July 10 results in Las Vegas:
- Miami Heat def. Milwaukee Bucks 119-86
- Brooklyn Nets def. New York Knicks 91-65 (Mikel Brown Jr.: 20 pts)
- Los Angeles Lakers def. OKC Thunder 96-84 (Adou Thiero 20 pts, Cameron Carr 18 pts)
- Boston Celtics def. Toronto Raptors 83-80 (OT) (Amari Williams 23/13)
- Memphis Grizzlies def. Chicago Bulls 97-96 (Boozer 23, Wilson 35)
- Phoenix Suns def. Portland Trail Blazers 81-79
- Houston Rockets def. Denver Nuggets 97-86
- Indiana Pacers def. Cleveland Cavaliers 99-93
- Liam McNeeley (Charlotte Hornets) exploded for 28 pts, 7-of-8 from three in 86-74 win over Orlando on July 9
- Zyon Pullin (Timberwolves): 24 pts/8 reb/12 ast in opener
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple box score sources confirm)
